tldr: Convert is a paid SaaS A/B testing and personalization platform; Togglz is an open-source Java library for feature toggles. Migration means dropping experimentation tooling and adopting an in-process JVM toggle library wired into your application code.
why_switch: Eliminate the $599/mo Convert subscription for teams that only need feature flags
Move flag evaluation in-process to remove network dependency on a SaaS
Adopt a permissive Apache 2.0 OSS library you can audit and self-host
Use type-safe Java enums for flag definitions instead of dashboard strings
Keep flag state inside your own database for full data ownership
what_to_export: List of active flags and their human-readable names
Flag descriptions, owners, and ticket references
Historical experiment results you want to archive
Any goal/metric definitions tied to experiments
export_method: Convert exposes flag and experiment data through its dashboard and reporting interface. Use the Convert UI to inventory all running and recent experiments, then export configuration and results to CSV or JSON where the dashboard supports it. For anything not covered by self-serve export, request an export from Convert support before cancelling. Capture screenshots of targeting trees and audience definitions, since complex segmentation may not survive a structured export. Save raw experiment results separately if you intend to keep historical analysis after the SaaS subscription ends. Treat the export as a one-time snapshot — once cancelled, regaining access is typically not free.
import_method: Togglz has no import tool — flags are defined in Java source. For each Convert flag, add a value to a Java enum implementing Togglz's Feature interface, annotated with metadata (label, default state, activation strategies). Choose a state repository (in-memory, JDBC, Redis, or filesystem) and a UserProvider for context. For percentage rollouts use the GradualActivationStrategy; for user/segment targeting use UsernameActivationStrategy or a custom ActivationStrategy. Wire the FeatureManager via Spring Boot starter or programmatic builder. Deploy the Togglz admin console behind authentication so non-engineers can flip flags at runtime. Re-create environment overrides via per-environment state repositories or property files. Validate each flag end-to-end in staging before flipping production traffic.

concept_mapping.note: Togglz handles toggling; statistical analysis must be added externally
Multi-arm experiments require custom strategies or external splitter
You implement matching logic against your user model

gotchas: Togglz is JVM-only; non-Java services that called Convert will need a different solution or a thin Togglz-backed HTTP wrapper
Statistical experimentation (significance testing, lift, CIs) is not part of Togglz — plan a separate analytics path
Multi-variant tests do not map cleanly; you may need custom strategies or to flatten experiments to single-toggle rollouts
Privacy-first cookieless tracking that Convert provided does not transfer; instrumentation must be re-implemented
Convert dashboard users will lose self-serve experiment creation — engineers must redeploy or use the Togglz console
Historical experiment data is locked to Convert once the subscription ends, so export everything you need first

email.subject: Heads up: we're moving feature flags off Convert to Togglz
email.body:
Hi team,

We're planning to move our feature flagging from Convert to Togglz, an open-source Java library, over the next 14 days. The goal is simpler ops, lower cost, and tighter integration with our JVM stack.

What changes for you:
- Flag toggles will move from the Convert dashboard to a self-hosted Togglz admin console.
- Flag definitions will live in our Java codebase as enums; new flags require a small PR.
- Experiment analytics will be handled by our existing analytics pipeline rather than Convert.

We'll run both systems in parallel for a short overlap, then sunset Convert. Please export anything you need from Convert (experiment results, audience definitions) before the cutover.

If any current experiment must keep running on Convert, reply by end of week so we can plan the timing.

Thanks,
Your customer
